schema_note: Declares how often each data type should be re-verified against its official source. Items with `retrieved` date older than the warn threshold are flagged in validator output (exit 2). Items older than the error threshold block the synthesise step (exit 1). Policy decisions are explicit + auditable — adjust here when AHPRA/DHA/uni pages shift policy cadence. v0.3.1 mechanism: every policy-driven value is now short-lived + auto-checked. Don't write policy numbers as static constants — always reference this file.

english_standard.rationale: AHPRA ELS / DHA English requirements can change with policy reviews. Should re-verify monthly against the official ELS page.

skills_assessment_process.rationale: OTC / AHPRA qualification assessment processes shift with major reforms (e.g. 27 Oct 2025 reform moved registration assessment from OTC to AHPRA/OTBA). Monthly check after any announced reform.

eoi_threshold.rationale: SkillSelect invitation rounds are quarterly. Between rounds, floor scores can shift ±5-10 points. Weekly check during invitation windows.

occupation_list.rationale: MLTSSL / ROL / SOL update with each program year (typically July). Monthly check after July program year start.

visa_allocation.rationale: State nomination allocations (190/491) can be exhausted mid-program-year without warning (e.g. NT 2025-26 closed 2026-01-26). Weekly check mandatory.

tuition_fee.rationale: Annual refresh in January (university calendar). Two-year tolerance for planning reports — by then the student would have applied.

course_facts.rationale: Course duration, intake months, PTE/IELTS, prereqs, scholarships shift year-to-year but rarely mid-year. Semester refresh.
applies_to_data_types: australian-courses.json (duration, intake, pte_*, ielts_*, prereq, scholarship)

policy_decision.rationale: Recent reforms (e.g. 27 Oct 2025 OTBA pathway, Dec 2025 AHPRA consultation closing 9 Feb 2026) — 2-week check after formalisation, then monthly.

register_data_static.rationale: Domain tier mappings and skill-selection data (QILT, AHPRA workforce stats) are slow-moving. 2-year check, 5-year error.

exit_codes.0: clean — all data within freshness thresholds + no PII hits + no source-tier issues
exit_codes.1: ERRORS — at least one data_type exceeds error threshold OR PII hit OR tier_5 social media source. Blocks synthesise step. Cannot push without remediation.
exit_codes.2: WARNINGS — at least one data_type exceeds warn threshold. Synthesise step continues but report must include staleness caveat. Push allowed with explicit override.

synthesise_gate_rule: Before generating the 6-section report, run `node bin/release-precheck.mjs`. If exit code is 1, refuse to synthesise. If exit code is 2, synthesise but add a 'data_staleness' footer listing which fields are past warn threshold. This is the auto-catch for AHPRA ELS changes (#1) and similar policy shifts — the next time the policy moves, the warn will fire automatically without code changes.
override_mechanism: If a known-good reason exists to push despite warnings (e.g. mid-program-year data is best available), the operator can document it in `data/known-stale.json` (not yet built — v0.3.2 backlog) with: {field, retrieved, override_reason, override_until, signed_by}. Until then, push with warnings requires explicit `--force` flag in release-precheck.
